prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a hask has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the hask-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three objects on a table: a hask, a florp, and a bep. The hask is underneath the florp. The florp is to-the-left-of the bep. The hask has a blue button on top, a red button on side, and a yellow button on bottom. The florp has a red button on top and a blue button on bottom. The bep has a yellow button on top and a blue button on side. Pressing the blue button on the hask activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three objects on a shelf: a thorp, a muk, and a prit. The prit is underneath the thorp. The thorp is to-the-left-of the muk. The thorp has a blue button on top, a blue button on side, and a red button on bottom. The muk has a yellow button on top and a red button on bottom. The prit has a blue button on top, a red button on side, and a yellow button on bottom.

Which object in the perception scenario is the hask-analog, and which button activates it?
